translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A man there hath been in the land of Uz -- Job his name -- and that man hath been perfect and upright -- both fearing God, and turning aside from evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there are borne to him seven sons and three daughters,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and his substance is seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred pairs of oxen, and five hundred she-asses, and a service very abundant; and that man is greater than any of the sons of the east.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his sons have gone and made a banquet -- the house of each [in] his day -- and have sent and called to their three sisters to eat and to drink with them;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and it cometh to pass, when they have gone round the days of the banquet, that Job doth send and sanctify them, and hath risen early in the morning, and caused to ascend burnt-offerings -- the number of them all -- for Job said, 'Perhaps my sons have sinned, yet blessed God in their heart.' Thus doth Job all the days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day is, that sons of God come in to station themselves by Jehovah, and there doth come also the Adversary in their midst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Whence comest thou?' And the Adversary answereth Jehovah and saith, 'From going to and fro in the land, and from walking up and down on it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Hast thou set thy heart against My servant Job because there is none like him in the land, a man perfect and upright, fearing God, and turning aside from evil?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Adversary answereth Jehovah and saith, 'For nought is Job fearing God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast not Thou made a hedge for him, and for his house, and for all that he hath -- round about?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The work of his hands Thou hast blessed, and his substance hath spread in the land, and yet, put forth, I pray Thee, Thy hand, and strike against anything that he hath -- if not: to Thy face he doth bless Thee!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Lo, all that he hath [is] in thy hand, only unto him put not forth thy hand.' And the Adversary goeth out from the presence of Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day is, that his sons and his daughters are eating, and drinking wine, in the house of their brother, the first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a messenger hath come in unto Job and saith, 'The oxen have been plowing, and the she-asses feeding by their sides,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Sheba doth fall, and take them, and the young men they have smitten by the mouth of the sword, and I am escaped -- only I alone -- to declare [it] to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While this [one] is speaking another also hath come and saith, 'Fire of God hath fallen from the heavens, and burneth among the flock, and among the young men, and consumeth them, and I am escaped -- only I alone -- to declare [it] to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While this [one] is speaking another also hath come and saith, 'Chaldeans made three heads, and rush on the camels, and take them, and the young men they have smitten by the mouth of the sword, and I am escaped -- only I alone -- to declare [it] to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While this [one] is speaking another also hath come and saith, 'Thy sons and thy daughters are eating, and drinking wine, in the house of their brother, the first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And lo, a great wind hath come from over the wilderness, and striketh against the four corners of the house, and it falleth on the young men, and they are dead, and I am escaped -- only I alone -- to declare [it] to thee.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job riseth, and rendeth his robe, and shaveth his head, and falleth to the earth, and doth obeisance,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he saith, 'Naked came I forth from the womb of my mother, and naked I turn back thither: Jehovah hath given and Jehovah hath taken: let the name of Jehovah be blessed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In all this Job hath not sinned, nor given folly to God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the day is, that sons of God come in to station themselves by Jehovah, and there doth come also the Adversary in their midst to station himself by Jehovah.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Whence camest thou?' And the Adversary answereth Jehovah and saith, 'From going to and fro in the land, and from walking up and down in it.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Hast thou set thy heart unto My servant Job because there is none like him in the land, a man perfect and upright, fearing God and turning aside from evil? and still he is keeping hold on his integrity, and thou dost move Me against him to swallow him up for nought!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Adversary answereth Jehovah and saith, 'A skin for a skin, and all that a man hath he doth give for his life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yet, put forth, I pray Thee, Thy hand, and strike unto his bone and unto his flesh -- if not: unto Thy face he doth bless Thee!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah saith unto the Adversary, 'Lo, he [is] in thy hand; only his life take care of.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Adversary goeth forth from the presence of Jehovah, and smiteth Job with a sore ulcer from the sole of his foot unto his crown.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he taketh to him a potsherd to scrape himself with it, and he is sitting in the midst of the ashes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his wife saith to him, 'Still thou art keeping hold on thine integrity: bless God and die.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he saith unto her, 'As one of the foolish women speaketh, thou speakest; yea, the good we receive from God, and the evil we do not receive.' In all this Job hath not sinned with his lips.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And three of the friends of Job hear of all this evil that hath come upon him, and they come in each from his place -- Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite -- and they are met together to come in to bemoan him, and to comfort him;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and they lift up their eyes from afar and have not discerned him, and they lift up their voice and weep, and rend each his robe, and sprinkle dust on their heads -- heavenward.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they sit with him on the earth seven days and seven nights, and there is none speaking unto him a word when they have seen that the pain hath been very great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After this hath Job opened his mouth, and revileth his day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the day perish in which I am born, And the night that hath said: 'A man-child hath been conceived.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That day -- let it be darkness, Let not God require it from above, Nor let light shine upon it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let darkness and death-shade redeem it, Let a cloud tabernacle upon it, Let them terrify it as the most bitter of days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That night -- let thick darkness take it, Let it not be united to days of the year, Into the number of months let it not come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo! that night -- let it be gloomy, Let no singing come into it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the cursers of day mark it, Who are ready to wake up Leviathan.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let the stars of its twilight be dark, Let it wait for light, and there is none, And let it not look on the eyelids of the dawn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because it hath not shut the doors Of the womb that was mine! And hide misery from mine eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why from the womb do I not die? From the belly I have come forth and gasp!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore have knees been before me? And what [are] breasts, that I suck?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For now, I have lain down, and am quiet, I have slept -- then there is rest to me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With kings and counsellors of earth, These building wastes for themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Or with princes -- they have gold, They are filling their houses [with] silver.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (Or as a hidden abortion I am not, As infants -- they have not seen light.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There the wicked have ceased troubling, And there rest do the wearied in power.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Together prisoners have been at ease, They have not heard the voice of an exactor,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Small and great [are] there the same. And a servant [is] free from his lord.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why giveth He to the miserable light, and life to the bitter soul?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who are waiting for death, and it is not, And they seek it above hid treasures.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who are glad -- unto joy, They rejoice when they find a grave.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To a man whose way hath been hidden, And whom God doth shut up?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For before my food, my sighing cometh, And poured out as waters [are] my roarings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a fear I feared and it meeteth me, And what I was afraid of doth come to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I was not safe -- nor was I quiet -- Nor was I at rest -- and trouble cometh!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath one tried a word with thee? -- Thou art weary! And to keep in words who is able?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, thou hast instructed many, And feeble hands thou makest strong.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The stumbling one do thy words raise up, And bowing knees thou dost strengthen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But now, it cometh in unto thee, And thou art weary; It striketh unto thee, and thou art troubled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not thy reverence thy confidence? Thy hope -- the perfection of thy ways?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember, I pray thee, Who, being innocent, hath perished? And where have the upright been cut off?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As I have seen -- ploughers of iniquity, And sowers of misery, reap it!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the breath of God they perish, And from the spirit of His anger consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The roaring of a lion, And the voice of a fierce lion, And teeth of young lions have been broken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An old lion is perishing without prey, And the whelps of the lioness do separate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And unto me a thing is secretly brought, And receive doth mine ear a little of it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In thoughts from visions of the night, In the falling of deep sleep on men,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fear hath met me, and trembling, And the multitude of my bones caused to fear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a spirit before my face doth pass, Stand up doth the hair of my flesh;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It standeth, and I discern not its aspect, A similitude [is] over-against mine eyes, Silence! and a voice I hear:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Is mortal man than God more righteous? Than his Maker is a man cleaner?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, in His servants He putteth no credence, Nor in His messengers setteth praise.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- the inhabitants of houses of clay, (Whose foundation [is] in the dust, They bruise them before a moth.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From morning to evening are beaten down, Without any regarding, for ever they perish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath not their excellency been removed with them? They die, and not in wisdom!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pray, call, is there any to answer thee? And unto which of the holy ones dost thou turn?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For provocation slayeth the perverse, And envy putteth to death the simple,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I have seen the perverse taking root, And I mark his habitation straightway,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Far are his sons from safety, And they are bruised in the gate, And there is no deliverer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whose harvest the hungry doth eat, And even from the thorns taketh it, And the designing swallowed their wealth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For sorrow cometh not forth from the dust, Nor from the ground springeth up misery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For man to misery is born, And the sparks go high to fly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yet I -- I inquire for God, And for God I give my word,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doing great things, and there is no searching. Wonderful, till there is no numbering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is giving rain on the face of the land, And is sending waters on the out-places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To set the low on a high place, And the mourners have been high [in] safety.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Making void thoughts of the subtile, And their hands do not execute wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Capturing the wise in their subtilty, And the counsel of wrestling ones was hastened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By day they meet darkness, And as night -- they grope at noon.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saveth the wasted from their mouth, And from a strong hand the needy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there is hope to the poor, And perverseness hath shut her mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, the happiness of mortal man, God doth reprove him: And the chastisement of the Mighty despise not,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He doth pain, and He bindeth up, He smiteth, and His hands heal.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In six distresses He delivereth thee, And in seven evil striketh not on thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In famine He hath redeemed thee from death, And in battle from the hands of the sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When the tongue scourgeth thou art hid, And thou art not afraid of destruction, When it cometh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At destruction and at hunger thou mockest, And of the beast of the earth, Thou art not afraid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (For with sons of the field [is] thy covenant, And the beast of the field Hath been at peace with thee.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast known that thy tent [is] peace, And inspected thy habitation, and errest not,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And hast known that numerous [is] Thy seed, And thine offspring as the herb of the earth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou comest in full age unto the grave, As the going up of a stalk in its season.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, this -- we searched it out -- it [is] right, hearken; And thou, know for thyself!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that my provocation were thoroughly weighed, And my calamity in balances They would lift up together!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For now, than the sands of the sea it is heavier, Therefore my words have been rash.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For arrows of the Mighty [are] with me, Whose poison is drinking up my spirit. Terrors of God array themselves [for] me!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Brayeth a wild ass over tender grass? Loweth an ox over his provender?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Eaten is an insipid thing without salt? Is there sense in the drivel of dreams?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul is refusing to touch! They [are] as my sickening food.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that my request may come, That God may grant my hope!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That God would please -- and bruise me, Loose His hand and cut me off!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet it is my comfort, (And I exult in pain -- He doth not spare,) That I have not hidden The sayings of the Holy One.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] my power that I should hope? And what mine end That I should prolong my life?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is my strength the strength of stones? Is my flesh brazen?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not my help with me, And substance driven from me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To a despiser of his friends [is] shame, And the fear of the Mighty he forsaketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My brethren have deceived as a brook, As a stream of brooks they pass away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That are black because of ice, By them doth snow hide itself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the time they are warm they have been cut off, By its being hot they have been Extinguished from their place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn aside do the paths of their way, They ascend into emptiness, and are lost.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Passengers of Tema looked expectingly, Travellers of Sheba hoped for them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They were ashamed that one hath trusted, They have come unto it and are confounded.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely now ye have become the same! Ye see a downfall, and are afraid.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it because I said, Give to me? And, By your power bribe for me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, Deliver me from the hand of an adversary? And, From the hand of terrible ones ransom me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Shew me, and I -- I keep silent, And what I have erred, let me understand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How powerful have been upright sayings, And what doth reproof from you reprove?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For reproof -- do you reckon words? And for wind -- sayings of the desperate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Anger on the fatherless ye cause to fall, And are strange to your friend.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, now, please, look upon me, Even to your face do I lie?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn back, I pray you, let it not be perverseness, Yea, turn back again -- my righteousness [is] in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is there in my tongue perverseness? Discerneth not my palate desirable things?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is there not a warfare to man on earth? And as the days of an hireling his days?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a servant desireth the shadow, And as a hireling expecteth his wage,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So I have been caused to inherit months of vanity, And nights of misery they numbered to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I lay down then I said, 'When do I rise!' And evening hath been measured, And I have been full of tossings till dawn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Clothed hath been my flesh [with] worms, And a clod of dust, My skin hath been shrivelled and is loathsome,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My days swifter than a weaving machine, And they are consumed without hope.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember Thou that my life [is] a breath, Mine eye turneth not back to see good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The eye of my beholder beholdeth me not. Thine eyes [are] upon me -- and I am not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Consumed hath been a cloud, and it goeth, So he who is going down to Sheol cometh not up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He turneth not again to his house, Nor doth his place discern him again.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also I -- I withhold not my mouth -- I speak in the distress of my spirit, I talk in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A sea -[monster] am I, or a dragon, That thou settest over me a guard?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When I said, 'My bed doth comfort me,' He taketh away in my talking my couch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast affrighted me with dreams, And from visions thou terrifiest me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my soul chooseth strangling, Death rather than my bones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have wasted away -- not to the age do I live. Cease from me, for my days [are] vanity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] man that Thou dost magnify him? And that Thou settest unto him Thy heart?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And inspectest him in the mornings, In the evenings dost try him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How long dost Thou not look from me? Thou dost not desist till I swallow my spittle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have sinned, what do I to Thee, O watcher of man? Why hast Thou set me for a mark to Thee, And I am for a burden to myself -- and what?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost not take away my transgression, And cause to pass away mine iniquity, Because now, for dust I lie down: And Thou hast sought me -- and I am not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when dost thou speak these things? And a strong wind -- sayings of thy mouth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth God pervert judgment? And doth the Mighty One pervert justice?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thy sons have sinned before Him, And He doth send them away, By the hand of their transgression,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou dost seek early unto God, And unto the Mighty makest supplication,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If pure and upright thou [art], Surely now He waketh for thee, And hath completed The habitation of thy righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thy beginning hath been small, And thy latter end is very great.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, ask I pray thee of a former generation, And prepare to a search of their fathers,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (For of yesterday we [are], and we know not, For a shadow [are] our days on earth.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Do they not shew thee -- speak to thee, And from their heart bring forth words?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Doth a rush wise without mire? A reed increase without water?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While it [is] in its budding -- uncropped, Even before any herb it withereth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So [are] the paths of all forgetting God, And the hope of the profane doth perish,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whose confidence is loathsome, And the house of a spider his trust.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He leaneth on his house -- and it standeth not: He taketh hold on it -- and it abideth not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Green he [is] before the sun, And over his garden his branch goeth out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By a heap his roots are wrapped, A house of stones he looketh for.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If [one] doth destroy him from his place, Then it hath feigned concerning him, I have not seen thee!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, this [is] the joy of his way, And from the dust others spring up.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, God doth not reject the perfect, Nor taketh hold on the hand of evil doers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: While he filleth with laughter thy mouth, And thy lips with shouting,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those hating thee do put on shame, And the tent of the wicked is not!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Truly I have known that [it is] so, And what -- is man righteous with God?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If he delight to strive with Him -- He doth not answer him one of a thousand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wise in heart and strong in power -- Who hath hardened toward Him and is at peace?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is removing mountains, And they have not known, Who hath overturned them in His anger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is shaking earth from its place, And its pillars move themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who is speaking to the sun, and it riseth not, And the stars He sealeth up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stretching out the heavens by Himself, And treading on the heights of the sea,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Making Osh, Kesil, and Kimah, And the inner chambers of the south.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doing great things till there is no searching, And wonderful, till there is no numbering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He goeth over by me, and I see not, And He passeth on, and I attend not to it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He snatches away, who bringeth it back? Who saith unto Him, 'What dost Thou?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God doth not turn back His anger, Under Him bowed have proud helpers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How much less do I -- I answer Him? Choose out my words with Him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whom, though I were righteous, I answer not, For my judgment I make supplication.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though I had called and He answereth me, I do not believe that He giveth ear [to] my voice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because with a tempest He bruiseth me, And hath multiplied my wounds for nought.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He permitteth me not to refresh my spirit, But filleth me with bitter things.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If of power, lo, the Strong One; And if of judgment -- who doth convene me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I be righteous, Mine mouth doth declare me wicked, Perfect I am! -- it declareth me perverse.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Perfect I am! -- I know not my soul, I despise my life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It is the same thing, therefore I said, 'The perfect and the wicked He is consuming.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If a scourge doth put to death suddenly, At the trial of the innocent He laugheth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Earth hath been given Into the hand of the wicked one. The face of its judges he covereth, If not -- where, who [is] he?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My days have been swifter than a runner, They have fled, they have not seen good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have passed on with ships of reed, As an eagle darteth on food.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though I say, 'I forget my talking, I forsake my corner, and I brighten up!'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have been afraid of all my griefs, I have known that Thou dost not acquit me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- I am become wicked; why [is] this? [In] vain I labour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have washed myself with snow-water, And purified with soap my hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then in corruption Thou dost dip me, And my garments have abominated me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But if a man like myself -- I answer him, We come together into judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If there were between us an umpire, He doth place his hand on us both.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth turn aside from off me his rod, And His terror doth not make me afraid,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I speak, and do not fear Him, But I am not right with myself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My soul hath been weary of my life, I leave off my talking to myself, I speak in the bitterness of my soul.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I say unto God, 'Do not condemn me, Let me know why Thou dost strive [with] me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it good for Thee that Thou dost oppress? That Thou despisest the labour of Thy hands, And on the counsel of the wicked hast shone?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Eyes of flesh hast Thou? As man seeth -- seest Thou?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the days of man [are] Thy days? Thy years as the days of a man?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That Thou inquirest for mine iniquity, And for my sin seekest?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou knowest that I am not wicked, And there is no deliverer from Thy hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy hands have taken pains about me, And they make me together round about, And Thou swallowest me up!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember, I pray Thee, That as clay Thou hast made me, And unto dust Thou dost bring me back.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost Thou not as milk pour me out? And as cheese curdle me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Skin and flesh Thou dost put on me, And with bones and sinews dost fence me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Life and kindness Thou hast done with me. And Thy inspection hath preserved my spirit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these Thou hast laid up in Thy heart, I have known that this [is] with Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I sinned, then Thou hast observed me, And from mine iniquity dost not acquit me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have done wickedly -- woe to me, And righteously -- I lift not up my head, Full of shame -- then see my affliction,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it riseth -- as a lion Thou huntest me. And Thou turnest back -- Thou shewest Thyself wonderful in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou renewest Thy witnesses against me, And dost multiply Thine anger with me, Changes and warfare [are] with me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And why from the womb Hast Thou brought me forth? I expire, and the eye doth not see me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As I had not been, I am, From the belly to the grave I am brought,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Are not my days few? Cease then, and put from me, And I brighten up a little,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before I go, and return not, Unto a land of darkness and death-shade,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A land of obscurity as thick darkness, Death-shade -- and no order, And the shining [is] as thick darkness.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zophar the Naamathite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is a multitude of words not answered? And is a man of lips justified?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy devices make men keep silent, Thou scornest, and none is causing blushing!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou sayest, 'Pure [is] my discourse, And clean I have been in Thine eyes.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet, O that God had spoken! And doth open His lips with thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And declare to thee secrets of wisdom, For counsel hath foldings. And know thou that God forgetteth for thee, [Some] of thine iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By searching dost thou find out God? Unto perfection find out the Mighty One?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Heights of the heavens! -- what dost thou? Deeper than Sheol! -- what knowest thou?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Longer than earth [is] its measure, And broader than the sea.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If He pass on, and shut up, and assemble, Who then dost reverse it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he hath known men of vanity, And He seeth iniquity, And one doth not consider [it]!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And empty man is bold, And the colt of a wild ass man is born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou -- thou hast prepared thy heart, And hast spread out unto Him thy hands,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If iniquity [is] in thy hand, put it far off, And let not perverseness dwell in thy tents.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For then thou liftest up thy face from blemish, And thou hast been firm, and fearest not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou dost forget misery, As waters passed away thou rememberest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And above the noon doth age rise, Thou fliest -- as the morning thou art.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast trusted because their is hope, And searched -- in confidence thou liest down,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast rested, And none is causing trembling, And many have entreated thy face;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the eyes of the wicked are consumed, And refuge hath perished from them, And their hope [is] a breathing out of soul!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Truly -- ye [are] the people, And with you doth wisdom die.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I also have a heart like you, I am not fallen more than you, And with whom is there not like these?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A laughter to his friend I am: 'He calleth to God, and He answereth him,' A laughter [is] the perfect righteous one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A torch -- despised in the thoughts of the secure Is prepared for those sliding with the feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At peace are the tents of spoilers, And those provoking God have confidence, He into whose hand God hath brought.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet, ask, I pray thee, [One of] the beasts, and it doth shew thee, And a fowl of the heavens, And it doth declare to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Or talk to the earth, and it sheweth thee, And fishes of the sea recount to thee:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who hath not known in all these, That the hand of Jehovah hath done this?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In whose hand [is] the breath of every living thing, And the spirit of all flesh of man.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth not the ear try words? And the palate taste food for itself?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With the very aged [is] wisdom, And [with] length of days understanding.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With Him [are] wisdom and might, To him [are] counsel and understanding.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He breaketh down, and it is not built up, He shutteth against a man, And it is not opened.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He keepeth in the waters, and they are dried up, And he sendeth them forth, And they overturn the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With Him [are] strength and wisdom, His the deceived and deceiver.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Causing counsellors to go away a spoil, And judges He maketh foolish.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The bands of kings He hath opened, And He bindeth a girdle on their loins.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Causing ministers to go away a spoil And strong ones He overthroweth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turning aside the lip of the stedfast, And the reason of the aged He taketh away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pouring contempt upon princes, And the girdle of the mighty He made feeble.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Removing deep things out of darkness, And He bringeth out to light death-shade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Magnifying the nations, and He destroyeth them, Spreading out the nations, and He quieteth them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turning aside the heart Of the heads of the people of the land, And he causeth them to wander In vacancy -- no way!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They feel darkness, and not light, He causeth them to wander as a drunkard.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, all -- hath mine eye seen, Heard hath mine ear, and it attendeth to it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: According to your knowledge I have known -- also I. I am not fallen more than you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yet I for the Mighty One do speak, And to argue for God I delight.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet, ye [are] forgers of falsehood, Physicians of nought -- all of you,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that ye would keep perfectly silent, And it would be to you for wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, I pray you, my argument, And to the pleadings of my lips attend,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For God do ye speak perverseness? And for Him do ye speak deceit?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His face do ye accept, if for God ye strive?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is [it] good that He doth search you, If, as one mocketh at a man, ye mock at Him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth surely reprove you, if in secret ye accept faces.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth not His excellency terrify you? And His dread fall upon you?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Your remembrances [are] similes of ashes, For high places of clay your heights.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Keep silent from me, and I speak, And pass over me doth what?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth? And my soul put in my hand?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He doth slay me -- I wait not! Only, my ways unto His face I argue.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- He [is] to me for salvation, For the profane cometh not before Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear ye diligently my word, And my declaration with your ears.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I pray you, I have set in order the cause, I have known that I am righteous.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] he that doth strive with me? For now I keep silent and gasp.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only two things, O God, do with me: Then from Thy face I am not hidden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy hand put far off from me, And Thy terror let not terrify me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And call Thou, and I -- I answer, Or -- I speak, and answer Thou me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How many iniquities and sins have I? My transgression and my sin let me know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why dost Thou hide Thy face? And reckonest me for an enemy to Thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A leaf driven away dost Thou terrify? And the dry stubble dost Thou pursue?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Thou writest against me bitter things, And causest me to possess iniquities of my youth:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And puttest in the stocks my feet, And observest all my paths, On the roots of my feet Thou settest a print,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he, as a rotten thing, weareth away, As a garment hath a moth consumed him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Man, born of woman! Of few days, and full of trouble!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a flower he hath gone forth, and is cut off, And he fleeth as a shadow and standeth not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- on this Thou hast opened Thine eyes, And dost bring me into judgment with Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who giveth a clean thing out of an unclean? not one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If determined are his days, The number of his months [are] with Thee, His limit Thou hast made, And he passeth not over;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Look away from off him that he may cease, Till he enjoy as an hireling his day.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For there is of a tree hope, if it be cut down, That again it doth change, That its tender branch doth not cease.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If its root becometh old in the earth, And its stem doth die in the dust,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the fragrance of water it doth flourish, And hath made a crop as a plant.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And a man dieth, and becometh weak, And man expireth, and where [is] he?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Waters have gone away from a sea, And a river becometh waste and dry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And man hath lain down, and riseth not, Till the wearing out of the heavens they awake not, Nor are roused from their sleep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that in Sheol Thou wouldest conceal me, Hide me till the turning of Thine anger, Set for me a limit, and remember me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If a man dieth -- doth he revive? All days of my warfare I wait, till my change come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost call, and I -- I answer Thee; To the work of Thy hands Thou hast desire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But now, my steps Thou numberest, Thou dost not watch over my sin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sealed up in a bag [is] my transgression, And Thou sewest up mine iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet, a falling mountain wasteth away, And a rock is removed from its place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stones have waters worn away, Their outpourings wash away the dust of earth, And the hope of man Thou hast destroyed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou prevailest [over] him for ever, and he goeth, He is changing his countenance, And Thou sendest him away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Honoured are his sons, and he knoweth not; And they are little, and he attendeth not to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only -- his flesh for him is pained, And his soul for him doth mourn.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth a wise man answer [with] vain knowledge? And fill [with] an east wind his belly?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To reason with a word not useful? And speeches -- no profit in them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, thou dost make reverence void, And dost diminish meditation before God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thy mouth teacheth thine iniquity, And thou chooseth the tongue of the subtile.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thy mouth declareth thee wicked, and not I, And thy lips testify against thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The first man art thou born? And before the heights wast thou formed?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of the secret counsel of God dost thou hear? And withdrawest thou unto thee wisdom?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What hast thou known, and we know not? Understandest thou -- and it is not with us?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Both the gray-headed And the very aged [are] among us -- Greater than thy father [in] days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Too few for thee are the comforts of God? And a gentle word [is] with thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What -- doth thine heart take thee away? And what -- are thine eyes high?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou turnest against God thy spirit? And hast brought out words from thy mouth:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] man that he is pure, And that he is righteous, one born of woman?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, in His holy ones He putteth no credence, And the heavens have not been pure in His eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- surely abominable and filthy Is man drinking as water perverseness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I shew thee -- hearken to me -- And this I have seen and declare:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Which the wise declare -- And have not hid -- from their fathers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To them alone was the land given, And a stranger passed not over into their midst:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'All days of the wicked he is paining himself, And few years have been laid up for the terrible one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A fearful voice [is] in his ears, In peace doth a destroyer come to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He believeth not to return from darkness, And watched [is] he for the sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is wandering for bread -- 'Where [is] it?' He hath known that ready at his hand Is a day of darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Terrify him do adversity and distress, They prevail over him As a king ready for a boaster.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he stretched out against God his hand, And against the Mighty he maketh himself mighty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He runneth unto Him with a neck, With thick bosses of his shields.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he hath covered his face with his fat, And maketh vigour over [his] confidence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he inhabiteth cities cut off, houses not dwelt in, That have been ready to become heaps.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is not rich, nor doth his wealth rise, Nor doth he stretch out on earth their continuance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He turneth not aside from darkness, His tender branch doth a flame dry up, And he turneth aside at the breath of His mouth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let him not put credence in vanity, He hath been deceived, For vanity is his recompence.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not in his day is it completed, And his bending branch is not green.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He shaketh off as a vine his unripe fruit, And casteth off as an olive his blossom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the company of the profane [is] gloomy, And fire hath consumed tents of bribery.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To conceive misery, and to bear iniquity, Even their heart doth prepare deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I have heard many such things, Miserable comforters [are] ye all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is there an end to words of wind? Or what doth embolden thee that thou answerest?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I also, like you, might speak, If your soul were in my soul's stead. I might join against you with words, And nod at you with my head.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I might harden you with my mouth, And the moving of my lips might be sparing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I speak, my pain is not restrained, And I cease -- what goeth from me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only, now, it hath wearied me; Thou hast desolated all my company,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Thou dost loathe me, For a witness it hath been, And rise up against me doth my failure, In my face it testifieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His anger hath torn, and he hateth me, He hath gnashed at me with his teeth, My adversary sharpeneth his eyes for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have gaped on me with their mouth, In reproach they have smitten my cheeks, Together against me they set themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God shutteth me up unto the perverse, And to the hands of the wicked turneth me over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At ease I have been, and he breaketh me, And he hath laid hold on my neck, And he breaketh me in pieces, And he raiseth me to him for a mark.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Go round against me do his archers. He splitteth my reins, and spareth not, He poureth out to the earth my gall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He breaketh me -- breach upon breach, He runneth upon me as a mighty one.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sackcloth I have sewed on my skin, And have rolled in the dust my horn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My face is foul with weeping, And on mine eyelids [is] death-shade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not for violence in my hands, And my prayer [is] pure.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O earth, do not thou cover my blood! And let there not be a place for my cry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also, now, lo, in the heavens [is] my witness, And my testifier in the high places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My interpreter [is] my friend, Unto God hath mine eye dropped:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he reasoneth for a man with God, And a son of man for his friend.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When a few years do come, Then a path I return not do I go.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My spirit hath been destroyed, My days extinguished -- graves [are] for me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If not -- mockeries [are] with me. And in their provocations mine eye lodgeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Place, I pray Thee, my pledge with Thee; Who is he that striketh hand with me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For their heart Thou hast hidden From understanding, Therefore Thou dost not exalt them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a portion he sheweth friendship, And the eyes of his sons are consumed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he set me up for a proverb of the peoples, And a wonder before them I am.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And dim from sorrow is mine eye, And my members as a shadow all of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Astonished are the upright at this, And the innocent against the profane Stirreth himself up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the righteous layeth hold [on] his way, And the clean of hands addeth strength, And -- dumb are they all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Return, and come in, I pray you, And I find not among you a wise man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My days have passed by, My devices have been broken off, The possessions of my heart!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Night for day they appoint, Light [is] near because of darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I wait -- Sheol [is] my house, In darkness I have spread out my couch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To corruption I have called: -- 'Thou [art] my father.' 'My mother' and 'my sister' -- to the worm.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And where [is] now my hope? Yea, my hope, who doth behold it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: [To] the parts of Sheol ye go down, If together on the dust we may rest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When do ye set an end to words? Consider ye, and afterwards do we speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore have we been reckoned as cattle? We have been defiled in your eyes!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (He is tearing himself in his anger.) For thy sake is earth forsaken? And removed is a rock from its place?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also, the light of the wicked is extinguished. And there doth not shine a spark of his fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The light hath been dark in his tent, And his lamp over him is extinguished.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Straitened are the steps of his strength, And cast him down doth his own counsel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he is sent into a net by his own feet, And on a snare he doth walk habitually.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Seize on the heel doth a gin, Prevail over him do the designing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hidden in the earth is his cord, And his trap on the path.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Round about terrified him have terrors, And they have scattered him -- at his feet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hungry is his sorrow, And calamity is ready at his side.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It consumeth the parts of his skin, Consume his parts doth death's first-born.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Drawn from his tent is his confidence, And it causeth him to step to the king of terrors.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It dwelleth in his tent -- out of his provender, Scattered over his habitation is sulphur.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From beneath his roots are dried up, And from above cut off is his crop.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His memorial hath perished from the land, And he hath no name on the street.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They thrust him from light unto darkness, And from the habitable earth cast him out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath no continuator, Nor successor among his people, And none is remaining in his dwellings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At this day westerns have been astonished And easterns have taken fright.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Only these [are] tabernacles of the perverse, And this the place God hath not known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Till when do ye afflict my soul, And bruise me with words?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: These ten times ye put me to shame, ye blush not. Ye make yourselves strange to me --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And also -- truly, I have erred, With me doth my error remain.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If, truly, over me ye magnify yourselves, And decide against me my reproach;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Know now, that God turned me upside down, And His net against me hath set round,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I cry out -- violence, and am not answered, I cry aloud, and there is no judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My way He hedged up, and I pass not over, And on my paths darkness He placeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mine honour from off me He hath stripped, And He turneth the crown from my head.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He breaketh me down round about, and I go, And removeth like a tree my hope.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He kindleth against me His anger, And reckoneth me to Him as His adversaries.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Come in do His troops together, And they raise up against me their way, And encamp round about my tent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My brethren from me He hath put far off, And mine acquaintances surely Have been estranged from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Ceased have my neighbours And my familiar friends have forgotten me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sojourners of my house and my maids, For a stranger reckon me: An alien I have been in their eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To my servant I have called, And he doth not answer, With my mouth I make supplication to him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My spirit is strange to my wife, And my favours to the sons of my [mother's] womb.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also sucklings have despised me, I rise, and they speak against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Abominate me do all the men of my counsel, And those I have loved, Have been turned against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To my skin and to my flesh Cleaved hath my bone, And I deliver myself with the skin of my teeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pity me, pity me, ye my friends, For the hand of God hath stricken against me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Why do you pursue me as God? And with my flesh are not satisfied?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth grant now, That my words may be written? Who doth grant that in a book they may be graven?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With a pen of iron and lead -- For ever in a rock they may be hewn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That -- I have known my Redeemer, The Living and the Last, For the dust he doth rise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And after my skin hath compassed this [body], Then from my flesh I see God:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whom I -- I see on my side, And mine eyes have beheld, and not a stranger, Consumed have been my reins in my bosom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: But ye say, 'Why do we pursue after him?' And the root of the matter hath been found in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Be ye afraid because of the sword, For furious [are] the punishments of the sword, That ye may know that [there is] a judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Zophar the Naamathite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore my thoughts cause me to answer, And because of my sensations in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The chastisement of my shame I hear, And the spirit of mine understanding Doth cause me to answer:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This hast thou known from antiquity? Since the placing of man on earth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That the singing of the wicked [is] short, And the joy of the profane for a moment,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though his excellency go up to the heavens, And his head against a cloud he strike --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As his own dung for ever he doth perish, His beholders say: 'Where [is] he?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a dream he fleeth, and they find him not, And he is driven away as a vision of the night,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The eye hath not seen him, and addeth not. And not again doth his place behold him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His sons do the poor oppress, And his hands give back his wealth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His bones have been full of his youth, And with him on the dust it lieth down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Though he doth sweeten evil in his mouth, Doth hide it under his tongue,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath pity on it, and doth not forsake it, And keep it back in the midst of his palate,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His food in his bowels is turned, The bitterness of asps [is] in his heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wealth he hath swallowed, and doth vomit it. From his belly God driveth it out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gall of asps he sucketh, Slay him doth the tongue of a viper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He looketh not on rivulets, Flowing of brooks of honey and butter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He is giving back [what] he laboured for, And doth not consume [it]; As a bulwark [is] his exchange, and he exults not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he oppressed -- he forsook the poor, A house he hath taken violently away, And he doth not build it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he hath not known ease in his belly. With his desirable thing he delivereth not himself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is not a remnant to his food, Therefore his good doth not stay.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the fulness of his sufficiency he is straitened. Every perverse hand doth meet him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It cometh to pass, at the filling of his belly, He sendeth forth against him The fierceness of His anger, Yea, He raineth on him in his eating.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He fleeth from an iron weapon, Pass through him doth a bow of brass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One hath drawn, And it cometh out from the body, And a glittering weapon from his gall proceedeth. On him [are] terrors.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All darkness is hid for his treasures, Consume him doth a fire not blown, Broken is the remnant in his tent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Reveal do the heavens his iniquity, And earth is raising itself against him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remove doth the increase of his house, Poured forth in a day of His anger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This [is] the portion of a wicked man from God. And an inheritance appointed him by God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear ye diligently my word, And this is your consolation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Bear with me, and I speak, And after my speaking -- ye may deride.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I -- to man [is] my complaint? and if [so], wherefore May not my temper become short?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Turn unto me, and be astonished, And put hand to mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, if I have remembered, then I have been troubled. And my flesh hath taken fright.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore do the wicked live? They have become old, Yea, they have been mighty in wealth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their seed is established, Before their face with them, And their offspring before their eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their houses [are] peace without fear, Nor [is] a rod of God upon them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His bullock hath eaten corn, and doth not loath. His cow bringeth forth safely, And doth not miscarry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They send forth as a flock their sucklings, And their children skip,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They lift [themselves] up at timbrel and harp, And rejoice at the sound of an organ.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They wear out in good their days, And in a moment [to] Sheol go down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they say to God, 'Turn aside from us, And the knowledge of Thy ways We have not desired.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What [is] the Mighty One that we serve Him? And what do we profit when we meet with Him?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, not in their hand [is] their good, (The counsel of the wicked Hath been far from me.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How oft is the lamp of the wicked extinguished, And come on them doth their calamity? Pangs He apportioneth in His anger.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They are as straw before wind, And as chaff a hurricane hath stolen away,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God layeth up for his sons his sorrow, He giveth recompense unto him -- and he knoweth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His own eyes see his destruction, And of the wrath of the Mighty he drinketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For what [is] his delight in his house after him, And the number of his months cut off?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To God doth [one] teach knowledge, And He the high doth judge?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This [one] dieth in his perfect strength, Wholly at ease and quiet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His breasts have been full of milk, And marrow his bones doth moisten.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And this [one] dieth with a bitter soul, And have not eaten with gladness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Together -- on the dust they lie down, And the worm doth cover them over.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I have known your thoughts, And the devices against me ye do wrongfully.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For ye say, 'Where [is] the house of the noble? And where the tent -- The tabernacles of the wicked?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Have ye not asked those passing by the way? And their signs do ye not know?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That to a day of calamity is the wicked spared. To a day of wrath they are brought.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth declare to his face his way? And [for] that which he hath done, Who doth give recompence to him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
