translations.text: And he -- to the graves he is brought. And over the heap a watch is kept.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sweet to him have been the clods of the valley, And after him every man he draweth, And before him there is no numbering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And how do ye comfort me [with] vanity, And in your answers hath been left trespass?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Eliphaz the Temanite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To God is a man profitable, Because a wise man to himself is profitable?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it a delight to the Mighty One That thou art righteous? is it gain, That thou makest perfect thy ways?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of thy reverence Doth He reason [with] thee? He entereth with thee into judgment:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not thy wickedness abundant? And there is no end to thine iniquities.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou takest a pledge of thy brother for nought, And the garments of the naked Thou dost strip off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou causest not the weary to drink water, And from the hungry thou withholdest bread.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As to the man of arm -- he hath the earth, And the accepted of face -- he dwelleth in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Widows thou hast sent away empty, And the arms of the fatherless are bruised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore round about thee [are] snares, And trouble thee doth fear suddenly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Or darkness -- thou dost not see, And abundance of waters doth cover thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not God high [in] heaven? And see the summit of the stars, That they are high.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou hast said, 'What -- hath God known? Through thickness doth He judge?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thick clouds [are] a secret place to Him, And He doth not see;' And the circle of the heavens He walketh habitually,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The path of the age dost thou observe, That men of iniquity have trodden?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who have been cut down unexpectedly, A flood is poured out on their foundation.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those saying to God, 'Turn aside from us,' And what doth the Mighty One to them?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath filled their houses [with] good: (And the counsel of the wicked Hath been far from me.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See do the righteous and they rejoice, And the innocent mocketh at them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Surely our substance hath not been cut off, And their excellency hath fire consumed.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Acquaint thyself, I pray thee, with Him, And be at peace, Thereby thine increase [is] good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Receive, I pray thee, from His mouth a law, And set His sayings in thy heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou dost return unto the Mighty Thou art built up, Thou puttest iniquity far from thy tents.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: So as to set on the dust a defence, And on a rock of the valleys a covering.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the Mighty hath been thy defence, And silver [is] strength to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For then on the Mighty thou delightest thyself, And dost lift up unto God thy face,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost make supplication unto Him, And He doth hear thee, And thy vows thou completest.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And thou decreest a saying, And it is established to thee, And on thy ways hath light shone.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For they have made low, And thou sayest, 'Lift up.' And the bowed down of eyes he saveth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He delivereth the not innocent, Yea, he hath been delivered By the cleanness of thy hands.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- to-day [is] my complaint bitter, My hand hath been heavy because of my sighing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: O that I had known -- and I find Him, I come in unto His seat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I arrange before Him the cause, And my mouth fill [with] arguments.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I know the words He doth answer me, And understand what He saith to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the abundance of power doth He strive with me? No! surely He putteth [it] in me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There the upright doth reason with Him, And I escape for ever from my judge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, forward I go -- and He is not, And backward -- and I perceive him not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: [To] the left in His working -- and I see not, He is covered [on] the right, and I behold not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He hath known the way with me, He hath tried me -- as gold I go forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On His step hath my foot laid hold, His way I have kept, and turn not aside,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The command of His lips, and I depart not. Above my allotted portion I have laid up The sayings of His mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He [is] in one [mind], And who doth turn Him back? And His soul hath desired -- and He doth [it].

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He doth complete my portion, And many such things [are] with Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore, from His presence I am troubled, I consider, and am afraid of Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And God hath made my heart soft, And the Mighty hath troubled me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have not been cut off before darkness, And before me He covered thick darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore from the Mighty One Times have not been hidden, And those knowing Him have not seen His days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The borders they reach, A drove they have taken violently away, Yea, they do evil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The ass of the fatherless they lead away, They take in pledge the ox of the widow,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They turn aside the needy from the way, Together have hid the poor of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, wild asses in a wilderness, They have gone out about their work, Seeking early for prey, A mixture for himself -- food for young ones.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In a field his provender they reap, And the vineyard of the wicked they glean.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The naked they cause to lodge Without clothing. And there is no covering in the cold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the inundation of hills they are wet, And without a refuge -- have embraced a rock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They take violently away From the breast the orphan, And on the poor they lay a pledge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Naked, they have gone without clothing, And hungry -- have taken away a sheaf.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Between their walls they make oil, Wine-presses they have trodden, and thirst.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of enmity men do groan, And the soul of pierced ones doth cry, And God doth not give praise.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have been among rebellious ones of light, They have not discerned His ways, Nor abode in His paths.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At the light doth the murderer rise, He doth slay the poor and needy, And in the night he is as a thief.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the eye of an adulterer Hath observed the twilight, Saying, 'No eye doth behold me.' And he putteth the face in secret.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath dug in the darkness -- houses; By day they shut themselves up, They have not known light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When together, morning [is] to them death shade, When he discerneth the terrors of death shade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Light he [is] on the face of the waters, Vilified is their portion in the earth, He turneth not the way of vineyards.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Drought -- also heat -- consume snow-waters, Sheol [those who] have sinned.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Forget him doth the womb, Sweeten [on] him doth the worm, No more is he remembered, And broken as a tree is wickedness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Treating evil the barren [who] beareth not, And [to] the widow he doth no good,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And hath drawn the mighty by his power, He riseth, and none believeth in life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He giveth to him confidence, and he is supported, And his eyes [are] on their ways.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: High they were [for] a little, and they are not, And they have been brought low. As all [others] they are shut up, And as the head of an ear of corn cut off.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if not now, who doth prove me a liar, And doth make of nothing my word?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Bildad the Shuhite answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The rule and fear [are] with Him, Making peace in His high places.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is their [any] number to His troops? And on whom ariseth not His light?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And what? is man righteous with God? And what? is he pure -- born of a woman?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo -- unto the moon, and it shineth not, And stars have not been pure in His eyes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How much less man -- a grub, And the son of man -- a worm!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What -- thou hast helped the powerless, Saved an arm not strong!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: What -- thou hast given counsel to the unwise, And wise plans in abundance made known.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With whom hast thou declared words? And whose breath came forth from thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Rephaim are formed, Beneath the waters, also their inhabitants.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Naked [is] Sheol over-against Him, And there is no covering to destruction.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Stretching out the north over desolation, Hanging the earth upon nothing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Binding up the waters in His thick clouds, And the cloud is not rent under them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Taking hold of the face of the throne, Spreading over it His cloud.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A limit He hath placed on the waters, Unto the boundary of light with darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pillars of the heavens do tremble, And they wonder because of His rebuke.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By His power He hath quieted the sea, And by His understanding smitten the proud.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By His Spirit the heavens He beautified, Formed hath His hand the fleeing serpent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, these [are] the borders of His way, And how little a matter is heard of Him, And the thunder of His might Who doth understand?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job addeth to lift up his simile, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God liveth! He turned aside my judgment, And the Mighty -- He made my soul bitter.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For all the while my breath [is] in me, And the spirit of God in my nostrils.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My lips do not speak perverseness, And my tongue doth not utter deceit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Pollution to me -- if I justify you, Till I expire I turn not aside mine integrity from me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On my righteousness I have laid hold, And I do not let it go, My heart doth not reproach me while I live.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As the wicked is my enemy, And my withstander as the perverse.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For what [is] the hope of the profane, When He doth cut off? When God doth cast off his soul?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His cry doth God hear, When distress cometh on him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On the Mighty doth he delight himself? Call God at all times?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I shew you by the hand of God, That which [is] with the Mighty I hide not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, ye -- all of you -- have seen, And why [is] this -- ye are altogether vain?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This [is] the portion of wicked man with God, And the inheritance of terrible ones From the Mighty they receive.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If his sons multiply -- for them [is] a sword. And his offspring [are] not satisfied [with] bread.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His remnant in death are buried, And his widows do not weep.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If he heap up as dust silver, And as clay prepare clothing,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He prepareth -- and the righteous putteth [it] on, And the silver the innocent doth apportion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath built as a moth his house, And as a booth a watchman hath made.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Rich he lieth down, and he is not gathered, His eyes he hath opened, and he is not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Overtake him as waters do terrors, By night stolen him away hath a whirlwind.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Take him up doth an east wind, and he goeth, And it frighteneth him from his place,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it casteth at him, and doth not spare, From its hand he diligently fleeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It clappeth at him its hands, And it hisseth at him from his place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely there is for silver a source, And a place for the gold they refine;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Iron from the dust is taken, And [from] the firm stone brass.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: An end hath he set to darkness, And to all perfection he is searching, A stone of darkness and death-shade.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A stream hath broken out from a sojourner, Those forgotten of the foot, They were low, from man they wandered.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The earth! from it cometh forth bread, And its under-part is turned like fire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A place of the sapphire [are] its stones, And it hath dust of gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A path -- not known it hath a ravenous fowl, Nor scorched it hath an eye of the kite,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Nor trodden it have the sons of pride, Not passed over it hath the fierce lion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Against the flint he sent forth his hand, He overturned from the root mountains.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Among rocks, brooks he hath cleaved, And every precious thing hath his eye seen.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From overflowing floods he hath bound, And the hidden thing bringeth out [to] light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the wisdom -- whence is it found? And where [is] this, the place of understanding?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Man hath not known its arrangement, Nor is it found in the land of the living.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The deep hath said, 'It [is] not in me,' And the sea hath said, 'It is not with me.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gold is not given for it, Nor is silver weighed -- its price.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It is not valued with pure gold of Ophir, With precious onyx and sapphire,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not equal it do gold and crystal, Nor [is] its exchange a vessel of fine gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Corals and pearl are not remembered, The acquisition of wisdom [is] above rubies.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Not equal it doth the topaz of Cush, With pure gold it is not valued.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the wisdom -- whence doth it come? And where [is] this, the place of understanding?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It hath been hid from the eyes of all living. And from the fowl of the heavens It hath been hidden.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Destruction and death have said: 'With our ears we have heard its fame.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God hath understood its way, And He hath known its place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He to the ends of the earth doth look, Under the whole heavens He doth see,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To make for the wind a weight, And the waters He meted out in measure.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In His making for the rain a limit, And a way for the brightness of the voices,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then He hath seen and declareth it, He hath prepared it, and also searched it out,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He saith to man: -- 'Lo, fear of the Lord, that [is] wisdom, And to turn from evil [is] understanding.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job addeth to lift up his simile, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth make me as [in] months past, As [in] the days of God's preserving me?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In His causing His lamp to shine on my head, By His light I walk [through] darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As I have been in days of my maturity, And the counsel of God upon my tent.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When yet the Mighty One [is] with me. Round about me -- my young ones,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When washing my goings with butter, And the firm rock [is] with me rivulets of oil.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When I go out to the gate by the city, In a broad place I prepare my seat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Seen me have youths, and they, been hidden, And the aged have risen -- they stood up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Princes have kept in words, And a hand they place on their mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The voice of leaders hath been hidden, And their tongue to the palate hath cleaved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the ear heard, and declareth me happy, And the eye hath seen, and testifieth [to] me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I deliver the afflicted who is crying, And the fatherless who hath no helper.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The blessing of the perishing cometh on me, And the heart of the widow I cause to sing.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Righteousness I have put on, and it clotheth me, As a robe and a diadem my justice.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Eyes I have been to the blind, And feet to the lame [am] I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A father I [am] to the needy, And the cause I have not known I search out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I break the jaw-teeth of the perverse, And from his teeth I cast away prey.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I say, 'With my nest I expire, And as the sand I multiply days.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My root is open unto the waters, And dew doth lodge on my branch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My honour [is] fresh with me, And my bow in my hand is renewed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To me they have hearkened, Yea, they wait, and are silent for my counsel.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After my word they change not, And on them doth my speech drop,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they wait as [for] rain for me, And their mouth they have opened wide [As] for the latter rain.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I laugh unto them -- they give no credence, And the light of my face cause not to fall.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I choose their way, and sit head, And I dwell as a king in a troop, When mourners he doth comfort.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, laughed at me, Have the younger in days than I, Whose fathers I have loathed to set With the dogs of my flock.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also -- the power of their hands, why [is it] to me? On them hath old age perished.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With want and with famine gloomy, Those fleeing to a dry place, Formerly a desolation and waste,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Those cropping mallows near a shrub, And broom-roots [is] their food.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the midst they are cast out, (They shout against them as a thief),

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In a frightful place of valleys to dwell, Holes of earth and clefts.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Among shrubs they do groan, Under nettles they are gathered together.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Sons of folly -- even sons without name, They have been smitten from the land.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, their song I have been, And I am to them for a byword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have abominated me, They have kept far from me, And from before me have not spared to spit.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because His cord He loosed and afflicteth me, And the bridle from before me, They have cast away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On the right hand doth a brood arise, My feet they have cast away, And they raise up against me, Their paths of calamity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They have broken down my path, By my calamity they profit, 'He hath no helper.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a wide breach they come, Under the desolation have rolled themselves.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath turned against me terrors, It pursueth as the wind mine abundance, And as a thick cloud, Hath my safety passed away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, in me my soul poureth itself out, Seize me do days of affliction.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At night my bone hath been pierced in me, And mine eyelids do not lie down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the abundance of power, Is my clothing changed, As the mouth of my coat it doth gird me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Casting me into mire, And I am become like dust and ashes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I cry unto Thee, And Thou dost not answer me, I have stood, and Thou dost consider me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou art turned to be fierce to me, With the strength of Thy hand, Thou oppressest me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost lift me up, On the wind Thou dost cause me to ride, And Thou meltest -- Thou levellest me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have known To death Thou dost bring me back, And [to] the house appointed for all living.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely not against the heap Doth He send forth the hand, Though in its ruin they have safety.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Did not I weep for him whose day is hard? Grieved hath my soul for the needy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When good I expected, then cometh evil, And I wait for light, and darkness cometh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My bowels have boiled, and have not ceased, Gone before me have days of affliction.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Mourning I have gone without the sun, I have risen, in an assembly I cry.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A brother I have been to dragons, And a companion to daughters of the ostrich.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My skin hath been black upon me, And my bone hath burned from heat,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my harp doth become mourning, And my organ the sound of weeping.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A covenant I made for mine eyes, And what -- do I attend to a virgin?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And what [is] the portion of God from above? And the inheritance of the Mighty from the heights?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is not calamity to the perverse? And strangeness to workers of iniquity?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth not He see my ways, And all my steps number?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have walked with vanity, And my foot doth hasten to deceit,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth weigh me in righteous balances, And God doth know my integrity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If my step doth turn aside from the way, And after mine eyes hath my heart gone, And to my hands cleaved hath blemish,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let me sow -- and another eat, And my products let be rooted out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If my heart hath been enticed by woman, And by the opening of my neighbour I laid wait,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Grind to another let my wife, And over her let others bend.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For it [is] a wicked thing, and a judicial iniquity;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a fire it [is], to destruction it consumeth, And among all mine increase doth take root,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I despise the cause of my man-servant, And of my handmaid, In their contending with me,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then what do I do when God ariseth? And when He doth inspect, What do I answer Him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Did not He that made me in the womb make him? Yea, prepare us in the womb doth One.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I withhold from pleasure the poor, And the eyes of the widow do consume,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I do eat my morsel by myself, And the orphan hath not eat of it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (But from my youth He grew up with me as [with] a father, And from the belly of my mother I am led.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I see [any] perishing without clothing, And there is no covering to the needy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If his loins have not blessed me, And from the fleece of my sheep He doth not warm himself,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have waved at the fatherless my hand, When I see in [him] the gate of my court,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My shoulder from its blade let fall, And mine arm from the bone be broken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a dread unto me [is] calamity [from] God, And because of His excellency I am not able.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have made gold my confidence, And to the pure gold have said, 'My trust,'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I rejoice because great [is] my wealth, And because abundance hath my hand found,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I see the light when it shineth, And the precious moon walking,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And my heart is enticed in secret, And my hand doth kiss my mouth,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It also [is] a judicial iniquity, For I had lied to God above.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I rejoice at the ruin of my hater, And stirred up myself when evil found him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, I have not suffered my mouth to sin, To ask with an oath his life.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If not -- say ye, O men of my tent, 'O that we had of his flesh, we are not satisfied.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the street doth not lodge a stranger, My doors to the traveller I open.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If I have covered as Adam my transgressions, To hide in my bosom mine iniquity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because I fear a great multitude, And the contempt of families doth affright me, Then I am silent, I go not out of the opening.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who giveth to me a hearing? lo, my mark. The Mighty One doth answer me, And a bill hath mine adversary written.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If not -- on my shoulder I take it up, I bind it a crown on myself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The number of my steps I tell Him, As a leader I approach Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If against me my land doth cry out, And together its furrows weep,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If its strength I consumed without money, And the life of its possessors, I have caused to breathe out,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Instead of wheat let a thorn go forth, And instead of barley a useless weed! The words of Job are finished.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And these three men cease from answering Job, for he [is] righteous in his own eyes,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and burn doth the anger of Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram; against Job hath his anger burned, because of his justifying himself more than God;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and against his three friends hath his anger burned, because that they have not found an answer, and condemn Job.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu hath waited earnestly beside Job with words, for they are older than he in days.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu seeth that there is no answer in the mouth of the three men, and his anger burneth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite answereth and saith: -- Young I [am] in days, and ye [are] age Therefore I have feared, And am afraid of shewing you my opinion.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I said: Days do speak, And multitude of years teach wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely a spirit is in man, And the breath of the Mighty One Doth cause them to understand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The multitude are not wise, Nor do the aged understand judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore I have said: Hearken to me, I do shew my opinion -- even I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I have waited for your words, I give ear unto your reasons, Till ye search out sayings.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And unto you I attend, And lo, there is no reasoner for Job, [Or] answerer of his sayings among you.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lest ye say, We have found wisdom, God doth thrust him away, not man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath not set in array words for me, And with your sayings I do not answer him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (They have broken down, They have not answered again, They removed from themselves words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I have waited, but they do not speak, For they have stood still, They have not answered any more.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I answer, even I -- my share, I shew my opinion -- even I.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have been full of words, Distressed me hath the spirit of my breast,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, my breast [is] as wine not opened, Like new bottles it is broken up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I speak, and there is refreshment to me, I open my lips and answer.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let me not, I pray you, accept the face of any, Nor unto man give flattering titles,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For I have not known to give flattering titles, In a little doth my Maker take me away.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And yet, I pray thee, O Job, Hear my speech and [to] all my words give ear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I pray thee, I have opened my mouth, My tongue hath spoken in the palate.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Of the uprightness of my heart [are] my sayings, And knowledge have my lips clearly spoken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Spirit of God hath made me, And the breath of the Mighty doth quicken me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou art able -- answer me, Set in array before me -- station thyself.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I [am], according to thy word, for God, From the clay I -- I also, have been formed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, my terror doth not frighten thee, And my burden on thee is not heavy.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely -- thou hast said in mine ears, And the sounds of words I hear:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Pure [am] I, without transgression, Innocent [am] I, and I have no iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, occasions against me He doth find, He doth reckon me for an enemy to Him,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth put in the stocks my feet, He doth watch all my paths.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, [in] this thou hast not been righteous, I answer thee, that greater is God than man.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Wherefore against Him hast thou striven, When [for] all His matters He answereth not?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For once doth God speak, and twice, (He doth not behold it.)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In a dream -- a vision of night, In the falling of deep sleep on men, In slumberings on a bed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then He uncovereth the ear of men, And for their instruction sealeth:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To turn aside man [from] doing, And pride from man He concealeth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He keepeth back his soul from corruption, And his life from passing away by a dart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath been reproved With pain on his bed, And the strife of his bones [is] enduring.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his life hath nauseated bread, And his soul desirable food.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His flesh is consumed from being seen, And high are his bones, they were not seen!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And draw near to the pit doth his soul, And his life to those causing death.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If there is by him a messenger, An interpreter -- one of a thousand, To declare for man his uprightness:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then He doth favour him and saith, 'Ransom him from going down to the pit, I have found an atonement.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Fresher [is] his flesh than a child's, He returneth to the days of his youth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He maketh supplication unto God, And He accepteth him. And he seeth His face with shouting, And He returneth to man His righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He looketh on men, and saith, 'I sinned, And uprightness I have perverted, And it hath not been profitable to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He hath ransomed my soul From going over into the pit, And my life on the light looketh.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, all these doth God work, Twice -- thrice with man,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To bring back his soul from the pit, To be enlightened with the light of the living.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Attend, O Job, hearken to me, Keep silent, and I -- I do speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If there are words -- answer me, Speak, for I have a desire to justify thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If there are not -- hearken thou to me, Keep silent, and I teach thee wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu answereth and saith:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear, O wise men, my words, And, O knowing ones, give ear to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the ear doth try words, And the palate tasteth to eat.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Judgment let us choose for ourselves, Let us know among ourselves what [is] good.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For Job hath said, 'I have been righteous, And God hath turned aside my right,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Against my right do I lie? Mortal [is] mine arrow -- without transgression.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] a man like Job? He drinketh scoffing like water,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath travelled for company With workers of iniquity, So as to go with men of wickedness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he hath said, 'It doth not profit a man, When he delighteth himself with God.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore, O men of heart, hearken to me; Far be it from God to do wickedness, And [from] the Mighty to do perverseness:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For the work of man he repayeth to him, And according to the path of each He doth cause him to find.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, truly, God doth not do wickedly, And the Mighty doth not pervert judgment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath inspected for Himself the earth? And who hath placed all the habitable world?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If He doth set on him His heart, His spirit and his breath unto Him He gathereth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Expire doth all flesh together, And man to dust returneth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if [there is] understanding, hear this, Give ear to the voice of my words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, doth one hating justice govern? Or the Most Just dost thou condemn?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath said to a king -- 'Worthless,' Unto princes -- 'Wicked?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That hath not accepted the person of princes, Nor hath known the rich before the poor, For a work of His hands [are] all of them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: [In] a moment they die, and at midnight Shake do people, and they pass away, And they remove the mighty without hand.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For His eyes [are] on the ways of each, And all his steps He doth see.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is no darkness nor death-shade, For workers of iniquity to be hidden there;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For He doth not suffer man any more, To go unto God in judgment,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He breaketh the mighty -- no searching! And He appointeth others in their stead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore He knoweth their works, And He hath overturned by night, And they are bruised.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As wicked He hath stricken them, In the place of beholders.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because that against right They have turned aside from after Him, And none of His ways have considered wisely,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To cause to come in unto Him The cry of the poor, And the cry of the afflicted He heareth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He giveth rest, and who maketh wrong? And hideth the face, and who beholdeth it? And in reference to a nation and to a man, [It is] the same.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the reigning of a profane man, From the snares of a people;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For unto God hath any said: 'I have taken away, I do not corruptly,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Besides [that which] I see, shew Thou me, If iniquity I have done -- I do not add?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By thee doth He recompense, That thou hast refused -- That thou dost choose, and not I? And what thou hast known, speak.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let men of heart say to me, And a wise man is hearkening to me.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Job -- not with knowledge doth he speak, And his words [are] not with wisdom.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: My Father! let Job be tried -- unto victory, Because of answers for men of iniquity,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For he doth add to his sin, Transgression among us he vomiteth, And multiplieth his sayings to God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu answereth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: This hast thou reckoned for judgment: Thou hast said -- 'My righteousness [is] more than God's?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For thou sayest, 'What doth it profit Thee! What do I profit from my sin?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I return thee words, and thy friends with thee,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Behold attentively the heavens -- and see, And behold the clouds, They have been higher than thou.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou hast sinned, what dost thou against Him? And thy transgressions have been multiplied, What dost thou to Him?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If thou hast been righteous, What dost thou give to Him? Or what from thy hand doth He receive?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For a man like thyself [is] thy wickedness, And for a son of man thy righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Because of the multitude of oppressions They cause to cry out, They cry because of the arm of the mighty.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And none said, 'Where [is] God my maker? Giving songs in the night,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Teaching us more than the beasts of the earth, Yea, than the fowl of the heavens He maketh us wiser.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There they cry, and He doth not answer, Because of the pride of evil doers.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Surely vanity God doth not hear, And the Mighty doth not behold it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, though thou sayest thou dost not behold Him, Judgment [is] before Him, and stay for Him.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And, now, because there is not, He hath appointed His anger, And He hath not known in great extremity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job [with] vanity doth open his mouth, Without knowledge words he multiplieth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Elihu addeth and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Honour me a little, and I shew thee, That yet for God [are] words.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I lift up my knowledge from afar, And to my Maker I ascribe righteousness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For, truly, my words [are] not false, The perfect in knowledge [is] with thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, God [is] mighty, and despiseth not, Mighty [in] power [and] heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He reviveth not the wicked, And the judgment of the poor appointeth;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He withdraweth not from the righteous His eyes, And [from] kings on the throne, And causeth them to sit for ever, and they are high,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if prisoners in fetters They are captured with cords of affliction,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Then He declareth to them their work, And their transgressions, Because they have become mighty,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He uncovereth their ear for instruction, And saith that they turn back from iniquity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: If they do hear and serve, They complete their days in good, And their years in pleasantness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And if they do not hearken, By the dart they pass away, And expire without knowledge.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the profane in heart set the face, They cry not when He hath bound them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Their soul dieth in youth, And their life among the defiled.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He draweth out the afflicted in his affliction, And uncovereth in oppression their ear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And also He moved thee from a strait place, [To] a broad place -- no straitness under it, And the sitting beyond of thy table Hath been full of fatness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And the judgment of the wicked thou hast fulfilled, Judgment and justice are upheld -- because of fury,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lest He move thee with a stroke, And the abundance of an atonement turn thee not aside.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth He value thy riches? He hath gold, and all the forces of power.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Desire not the night, For the going up of peoples in their stead.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Take heed -- do not turn unto iniquity, For on this thou hast fixed Rather than [on] affliction.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, God doth sit on high by His power, Who [is] like Him -- a teacher?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath appointed unto Him his way? And who said, 'Thou hast done iniquity?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Remember that thou magnify His work That men have beheld.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: All men have looked on it, Man looketh attentively from afar.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, God [is] high, And we know not the number of His years, Yea, there [is] no searching.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When He doth diminish droppings of the waters, They refine rain according to its vapour,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Which clouds do drop, They distil on man abundantly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, doth [any] understand The spreadings out of a cloud? The noises of His tabernacle?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, He hath spread over it His light, And the roots of the sea He hath covered,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For by them He doth judge peoples, He giveth food in abundance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By two palms He hath covered the light, And layeth a charge over it in meeting,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He sheweth by it [to] his friend substance, Anger against perversity.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Also, at this my heart trembleth, And it moveth from its place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hearken diligently to the trembling of His voice, Yea, the sound from His mouth goeth forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Under the whole heavens He directeth it, And its light [is] over the skirts of the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After it roar doth a voice -- He thundereth with the voice of His excellency, And He doth not hold them back, When His voice is heard.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: God thundereth with His voice wonderfully, Doing great things and we know not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For to snow He saith, 'Be [on] the earth.' And the small rain and great rain of His power.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Into the hand of every man he sealeth, For the knowledge by all men of His work.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And enter doth the beast into covert, And in its habitations it doth continue.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the inner chamber cometh a hurricane, And from scatterings winds -- cold,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the breath of God is frost given, And the breadth of waters is straitened,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Yea, by filling He doth press out a cloud, Scatter a cloud doth His light.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it is turning itself round by His counsels, For their doing all He commandeth them, On the face of the habitable earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whether for a rod, or for His land, Or for kindness -- He doth cause it to come.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hear this, O Job, Stand and consider the wonders of God.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou know when God doth place them, And caused to shine the light of His cloud?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou know the balancings of a cloud? The wonders of the Perfect in knowledge?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: How thy garments [are] warm, In the quieting of the earth from the south?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast made an expanse with Him For the clouds -- strong as a hard mirror!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Let us know what we say to Him, We set not in array because of darkness.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is it declared to Him that I speak? If a man hath spoken, surely he is swallowed up.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, they have not seen the light, Bright it [is] in the clouds, And the wind hath passed by and cleanseth them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From the golden north it cometh, Beside God [is] fearful honour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The Mighty! we have not found Him out, High in power and judgment, He doth not answer! And abundant in righteousness,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore do men fear Him, He seeth not any of the wise of heart.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah answereth Job out of the whirlwind, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who [is] this -- darkening counsel, By words without knowledge?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gird, I pray thee, as a man, thy loins, And I ask thee, and cause thou Me to know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Where wast thou when I founded earth? Declare, if thou hast known understanding.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who placed its measures -- if thou knowest? Or who hath stretched out upon it a line?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: On what have its sockets been sunk? Or who hath cast its corner-stone?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the singing together of stars of morning, And all sons of God shout for joy,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And He shutteth up with doors the sea, In its coming forth, from the womb it goeth out.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In My making a cloud its clothing, And thick darkness its swaddling band,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And I measure over it My statute, And place bar and doors,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And say, 'Hitherto come thou, and add not, And a command is placed On the pride of thy billows.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast thou commanded morning since thy days? Causest thou the dawn to know its place?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To take hold on the skirts of the earth, And the wicked are shaken out of it,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: It turneth itself as clay of a seal And they station themselves as clothed.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And withheld from the wicked is their light, And the arm lifted up is broken.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast thou come in to springs of the sea? And in searching the deep Hast thou walked up and down?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Revealed to thee were the gates of death? And the gates of death-shade dost thou see?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast understanding, Even unto the broad places of earth! Declare -- if thou hast known it all.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Where [is] this -- the way light dwelleth? And darkness, where [is] this -- its place?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That thou dost take it unto its boundary, And that thou dost understand The paths of its house.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast known -- for then thou art born And the number of thy days [are] many!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast thou come in unto the treasure of snow? Yea, the treasures of hail dost thou see?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: That I have kept back for a time of distress, For a day of conflict and battle.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Where [is] this, the way light is apportioned? It scattereth an east wind over the earth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath divided for the flood a conduit? And a way for the lightning of the voices?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To cause [it] to rain on a land -- no man, A wilderness -- no man in it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: To satisfy a desolate and waste place, And to cause to shoot up The produce of the tender grass?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hath the rain a father? Or who hath begotten the drops of dew?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From whose belly came forth the ice? And the hoar-frost of the heavens, Who hath begotten it?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As a stone waters are hidden, And the face of the deep is captured.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou bind sweet influences of Kimah? Or the attractions of Kesil dost thou open?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou bring out Mazzaroth in its season? And Aysh for her sons dost thou comfort?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast thou known the statutes of heaven? Or dost thou appoint Its dominion in the earth?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou lift up to the cloud thy voice, And abundance of water doth cover thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou send out lightnings, and they go And say unto thee, 'Behold us?'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath put in the inward parts wisdom? Or who hath given To the covered part understanding?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth number the clouds by wisdom? And the bottles of the heavens, Who doth cause to lie down,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In the hardening of dust into hardness, And clods cleave together?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou hunt for a lion prey? And the desire of young lions fulfil?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: When they bow down in dens -- Abide in a thicket for a covert?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who doth prepare for a raven his provision, When his young ones cry unto God? They wander without food.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hast thou known the time of The bearing of the wild goats of the rock? The bringing forth of hinds thou dost mark!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou dost number the months they fulfil? And thou hast known the time of their bringing forth!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They bow down, Their young ones they bring forth safely, Their pangs they cast forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Safe are their young ones, They grow up in the field, they have gone out, And have not returned to them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath sent forth the wild ass free? Yea, the bands of the wild ass who opened?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Whose house I have made the wilderness, And his dwellings the barren land,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth laugh at the multitude of a city, The cries of an exactor he heareth not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The range of mountains [is] his pasture, And after every green thing he seeketh.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is a Reem willing to serve thee? Doth he lodge by thy crib?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou bind a Reem in a furrow [with] his thick band? Doth he harrow valleys after thee?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou trust in him because great [is] his power? And dost thou leave unto him thy labour?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou trust in him That he doth bring back thy seed? And [to] thy threshing-floor doth gather [it]?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The wing of the rattling ones exulteth, Whether the pinion of the ostrich or hawk.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For she leaveth on the earth her eggs, And on the dust she doth warm them,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And she forgetteth that a foot may press it, And a beast of the field tread it down.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Her young ones it hath hardened without her, In vain [is] her labour without fear.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For God hath caused her to forget wisdom, And He hath not given a portion To her in understanding:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At the time on high she lifteth herself up, She laugheth at the horse and at his rider.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou give to the horse might? Dost thou clothe his neck [with] a mane?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou cause him to rush as a locust? The majesty of his snorting [is] terrible.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: They dig in a valley, and he rejoiceth in power, He goeth forth to meet the armour.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He laugheth at fear, and is not affrighted, And he turneth not back from the face of the sword.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Against him rattle doth quiver, The flame of a spear, and a halbert.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: With trembling and rage he swalloweth the ground, And remaineth not stedfast Because of the sound of a trumpet.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Among the trumpets he saith, Aha, And from afar he doth smell battle, Roaring of princes and shouting.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By thine understanding flieth a hawk? Spreadeth he his wings to the south?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: At thy command goeth an eagle up high? Or lifteth he up his nest?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A rock he doth inhabit, Yea, he lodgeth on the tooth of a rock, and fortress.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From thence he hath sought food, To a far off place his eyes look attentively,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And his brood gulp up blood, And where the pierced [are] -- there [is] he!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah doth answer Job, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Is the striver with the Mighty instructed? The reprover of God, let him answer it.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth Jehovah, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I have been vile, What do I return to Thee? My hand I have placed on my mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Once I have spoken, and I answer not, And twice, and I add not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah answereth Job out of the whirlwind, and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Gird, I pray thee, as a man, thy loins, I ask thee, and cause thou Me to know.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou also make void My judgment? Dost thou condemn Me, That thou mayest be righteous?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And an arm like God hast thou? And with a voice like Him dost thou thunder?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Put on, I pray thee, excellency and loftiness, Yea, honour and beauty put on.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Scatter abroad the wrath of thine anger, And see every proud one, and make him low.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: See every proud one -- humble him, And tread down the wicked in their place.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Hide them in the dust together, Their faces bind in secret.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And even I -- I do praise thee, For thy right hand giveth salvation to thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I pray thee, Behemoth, that I made with thee: Grass as an ox he eateth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, I pray thee, his power [is] in his loins, And his strength in the muscles of his belly.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He doth bend his tail as a cedar, The sinews of his thighs are wrapped together,

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His bones [are] tubes of brass, His bones [are] as a bar of iron.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He [is] a beginning of the ways of God, His Maker bringeth nigh his sword;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: For food do mountains bear for him, And all the beasts of the field play there.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Under shades he lieth down, In a secret place of reed and mire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Cover him do shades, [with] their shadow, Cover him do willows of the brook.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, a flood oppresseth -- he doth not haste, He is confident though Jordan Doth come forth unto his mouth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Before his eyes doth [one] take him, With snares doth [one] pierce the nose?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou draw leviathan with an angle? And with a rope thou lettest down -- his tongue?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou put a reed in his nose? And with a thorn pierce his jaw?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth he multiply unto thee supplications? Doth he speak unto thee tender things?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Doth he make a covenant with thee? Dost thou take him for a servant age-during?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou play with him as a bird? And dost thou bind him for thy damsels?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: (Feast upon him do companions, They divide him among the merchants!)

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Dost thou fill with barbed irons his skin? And with fish-spears his head?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Place on him thy hand, Remember the battle -- do not add!

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Lo, the hope of him is found a liar, Also at his appearance is not one cast down?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: None so fierce that he doth awake him, And who [is] he before Me stationeth himself?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath brought before Me and I repay? Under the whole heavens it [is] mine.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: I do not keep silent concerning his parts, And the matter of might, And the grace of his arrangement.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Who hath uncovered the face of his clothing? Within his double bridle who doth enter?

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The doors of his face who hath opened? Round about his teeth [are] terrible.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: A pride -- strong ones of shields, Shut up -- a close seal.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One unto another they draw nigh, And air doth not enter between them.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: One unto another they adhere, They stick together and are not separated.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His sneezings cause light to shine, And his eyes [are] as the eyelids of the dawn.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Out of his mouth do flames go, sparks of fire escape.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Out of his nostrils goeth forth smoke, As a blown pot and reeds.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His breath setteth coals on fire, And a flame from his mouth goeth forth.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: In his neck lodge doth strength, And before him doth grief exult.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The flakes of his flesh have adhered -- Firm upon him -- it is not moved.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: His heart [is] firm as a stone, Yea, firm as the lower piece.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: From his rising are the mighty afraid, From breakings they keep themselves free.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The sword of his overtaker standeth not, Spear -- dart -- and lance.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He reckoneth iron as straw, brass as rotten wood.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: The son of the bow doth not cause him to flee, Turned by him into stubble are stones of the sling.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: As stubble have darts been reckoned, And he laugheth at the shaking of a javelin.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Under him [are] sharp points of clay, He spreadeth gold on the mire.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: He causeth to boil as a pot the deep, The sea he maketh as a pot of ointment.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: After him he causeth a path to shine, One thinketh the deep to be hoary.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: There is not on the earth his like, That is made without terror.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Every high thing he doth see, He [is] king over all sons of pride.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job answereth Jehovah and saith: --

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Thou hast known that [for] all things Thou art able, And not withheld from Thee is [any] device:

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Who [is] this, hiding counsel without knowledge?' Therefore, I have declared, and understand not, Too wonderful for me, and I know not.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: 'Hear, I pray thee, and I -- I do speak, I ask thee, and cause thou me to know.'

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: By the hearing of the ear I heard Thee, And now mine eye hath seen Thee.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: Therefore do I loathe [it], And I have repented on dust and ashes.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And it cometh to pass after Jehovah's speaking these words unto Job, that Jehovah saith unto Eliphaz the Temanite, 'Burned hath Mine anger against thee, and against thy two friends, because ye have not spoken concerning Me rightly, like My servant Job.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And now, take to you seven bullocks and seven rams, and go ye unto My servant Job, and ye have caused a burnt-offering to ascend for you; and Job My servant doth pray for you, for surely his face I accept, so as not to do with you folly, because ye have not spoken concerning Me rightly, like My servant Job.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And they go -- Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, Zophar the Naamathite -- and do as Jehovah hath spoken unto them; and Jehovah doth accept the face of Job.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah hath turned [to] the captivity of Job in his praying for his friends, and Jehovah doth add [to] all that Job hath -- to double.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And come unto him do all his brethren, and all his sisters, and all his former acquaintances, and they eat bread with him in his house, and bemoan him, and comfort him concerning all the evil that Jehovah had brought upon him, and they gave to him each one kesitah, and each one ring of gold.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Jehovah hath blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning, and he hath fourteen thousand of a flock, and six thousand camels, and a thousand pairs of oxen, and a thousand she-asses.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And he hath seven sons and three daughters;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and he calleth the name of the one Jemima, and the name of the second Kezia, and the name of the third Keren-Happuch.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And there have not been found women fair as the daughters of Job in all the land, and their father doth give to them an inheritance in the midst of their brethren.

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: And Job liveth after this a hundred and forty years, and seeth his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations;

translations.name: Young's Literal Translation
translations.text: and Job dieth, aged and satisfied [with] days.
